chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, KARACHI Cr. B.A. No.2694 of 2025 (Zeeshan vs. The State) Present: Mr. Justice Muhammad Iqbal Kalhoro Mr. Justice Syed Fiaz-ul-Hassan Shah For hearing of bail application Date of hearing & order 25.11.2025 Mr. Muhammad Ibrahim Abro, advocate for applicant Mr. Ali Haider Saleem, Addl: PG Sindh O R D E R ----- Muhammad Iqbal Kalhoro, J:- Applicant was arrested on 26.07.2025 from Main Sea View Road, Khayaban-e-Bukhari, Phase-VI, DHA, Karachi by Police of Police Station Darakshan, Karachi, and from him 510 grams of charas was allegedly recovered. Hence, he was booked in the present FIR. 2. Learned counsel for the applicant has argued that applicant has been falsely implicated in this case and is no more required for further investigation as the challan has been submitted. He has added that the offence does not fall within the prohibitory clause u/s 497 Cr. PC and the applicant is a first offender. 3. Learned Addl: Prosecutor General Sindh has opposed the grant of bail; nonetheless, he could not dispute that the applicant is the first offender and he is no more required for further investigation and that the offence does not fall within the prohibitory clause u/s 497 Cr. PC. Accordingly, this application is allowed and applicant is granted bail subject to his furnishing a solvent surety in the sum of Rs.1,00,000/ (Rupees one lac) with P.R. bond in the like amount to be executed to the satisfaction of the trial Court. 4. The bail application is disposed of in the above terms; the findings made hereinabove are tentative in nature and would not prejudice case of either party at trial.

Accordingly, this application is allowed and applicant is granted bail subject to his furnishing a solvent surety in the sum of Rs.1,00,000/ (Rupees one lac) with P.R. bond in the like amount to be executed to the satisfaction of the trial Court. 4. The bail application is disposed of in the above terms; the findings made hereinabove are tentative in nature and would not prejudice case of either party at trial. JUDGE JUDGE Rafiq/PA.
